background.experience: reconstructing transaction chains after control failures
background.attention_bias: sequence, custody transitions, and acceptance evidence

readers.thesis: Build sovereignty exists when every accepted artifact has a reconstructable chain from admitted source to independently reproduced bytes, without relying on a provider success signal.
observations: Earlier runs separate execution from authority and require destructive recovery evidence.
The conversation repeatedly corrects claims that confuse an absence of active errors with proof of correctness.
uncertainties: The complete historical transcript and private archive were unavailable.
The current application has not yet produced two independently matching build outputs.

background.experience: cross-platform toolchain determinism and binary comparison
background.attention_bias: hidden inputs, undefined behavior, and environmental variance

readers.thesis: Deterministic execution requires controlling or declaring every input, then varying the environment aggressively enough to expose hidden dependencies before artifact acceptance.
observations: Sandboxing and derivation hashing constrain inputs but do not eliminate timestamps, randomness, CPU variance, or poisoned toolchains.
Signing belongs after reproducible output acceptance because signing can introduce nondeterminism.
uncertainties: Node and Vite dependency reproducibility remains unmeasured.
Cross-architecture byte equality may be infeasible for some outputs and requires declared platform scope.

agreements: Provider build status never authorizes artifact acceptance.
Two clean builders and exact digest agreement form the initial acceptance gate.
Reproducibility and source trust remain separate claims.
disagreements: Reader A prioritizes custody and acceptance evidence; Reader B prioritizes environment-variance coverage.
Reader A accepts two matching builds as a strong operational gate; Reader B treats two builds as only the start of a reproducibility campaign.
omissions_detected: No committed Nix flake exists for the current application.
No measured clean-build duration, mismatch rate, or dependency-mirror size exists.
No complete private corpus was available.
revisions_after_discussion: Require two clean builders plus explicit variance testing rather than two identical environments.
Record the platform scope of every reproducibility claim and quarantine mismatches for structured diagnosis.

notes_added.statement: Accept a build artifact only after independent clean executors reproduce the same bytes from pinned source, dependencies, toolchain, and build instructions.
Treat reproducibility as an artifact-identity gate, not proof that the source or pinned toolchain is safe.

notes_challenged.challenge: Disposable CI workers reduce persistence, but accepted artifacts still require independent byte reproduction and a separate artifact-identity gate.

privacy.review: Contains only abstract architecture conclusions, synthetic reader profiles, coverage boundaries, and non-identifying continuity findings. It excludes transcript excerpts, personal facts, health or animal details, credentials, locations, deleted text, and unique conversation fragments.
